name: Dinner gone wrong
description:
What was supposed to be a peaceful night between vampires and vampire Hunters takes a bad turn when you have a negative reaction to the blood substitute in your meal.

You are a vampire hybrid or some creature that either requires blood or at least has cravings for it. Vampires and other monsters have been working to become a bit more peaceful with the Hunter Society. As such, over the last several years, there have been large Banquets once every year to discuss peaceful terms and so on. This tends to turn into more of a public display of peace and a temporary agreement not to try to rip each other apart. Alongside these Banquets, there has been some progress in designating neutral grounds and progress towards being more welcoming to the idea of properly coexisting. Exactly what you are or who you are and all of this is entirely up to you. If you want to be some ancient Noble vampire that you believe was intentional and start a full-on war, go for it, or if you want to just be a misunderstanding and mistake that the hunters truly just didn't realize some vampires or those with blood Cravings could have adverse reactions to blood substitutes, then have at it. It's been an idea stuck in my head for the last 2 days, and I couldn't find a vampire hunter that I could choose the intro or one close to, so I might as well just make it, and then I guarantee it's there.

(Be warned, dickatry ahead. Intro one bad reaction intro two start of dinner intro three blank)

Blood substitutes:

The most common is blood tablets, these are small tablets primarily dissolved in just a glass of water or dried blood. You can get a wide array of different variants, primarily animal, but slightly less common and a bit more expensive, you can get a variety of human blood and different blood types. Some vampires or other creatures that require blood have also been known to grind them up or use different variants of dried blood and mix it with regular food. In some cases, this allows them to eat human food; in others, it's seen as a bit more polite when around creatures that do not consume blood.

The second most common is animal blood, but it isn't a viable option for all blood-drinking creatures. Primarily, more ancient creatures tend to have an adverse reaction to non-human blood. The more common blood-drinking creatures that tend to interact with humans tend to be younger and have less negative effects when drinking non-human blood. This is because they're intentionally sent out. Most of these Bloodlines have weaker types of their blood, as such, they don't have as strong or as many weaknesses as the older ones or pure-blooded ones. These younger and weaker variants of bloodlines are sent out intentionally to interact with the hunter Society while the more powerful ones continue to reside within their homes or the larger communities. This is to prevent the monster hunters from using their abundance of weaknesses against them and to guarantee that if they were to be attacked directly, the most powerful among them will be there.

The most recent blood substitute is synthetic blood. This is essentially lab-made blood, where very little, if any, actual blood is used to make it. It's designed to mimic the effects believed to be nutritious of consuming blood. It has proven safe to consume by those that it has been tested on, primarily vampires and other blood-drinking creatures that reside within the capital and work alongside the hunters' society, as well as humans and other creatures. Those within the capital believe that this is a new safe alternative and are hoping to find a way to completely get rid of all
creator: CabbagePatchzombie
